user_query:
I maintain public lab computers at our mosque and community center, and I want a friendly, multilingual login banner (motd-like) that promotes respect and counters Islamophobia. Please set up a small, self-contained workspace pipeline that does the following:

Inputs (provided in this workspace):
- Read quotes from input/quotes.json and settings from input/config.yaml.

Internet requirement:
- Download the Unicode Character Database text file named "UnicodeData.txt" from the official unicode.org domain (the standard UCD location on unicode.org) and save it to external/unicode/UnicodeData.txt. Do not hardcode any mirrors or non-official hosts.

Outputs (create these paths/files under the workspace):
1) out/banner/motd.txt — A plain-text banner that starts with the motd_header from input/config.yaml followed by exactly max_quotes entries from input/quotes.json. Each entry should be on its own line, formatted as: "- [<language>] <text> — <attribution>". Use the language field values from the JSON as the bracketed tag. If there are more than max_quotes available, choose any mix but include at least one Arabic (ar) and one English (en) entry if both exist in the input file.
2) out/reports/char_report.json — A JSON report analyzing the characters used in each selected quote. For each included quote, record: {"id": <id>, "unique_codepoints_hex": ["U+...."], "general_category_counts": {"Lu": x, "Ll": y, ...}, "unmapped_characters": ["..."]}. Use UnicodeData.txt to (a) verify that each character is assigned in Unicode, (b) extract the General Category for each character (field 3 in UnicodeData.txt), and (c) list any characters you could not resolve in "unmapped_characters" (should be empty for valid characters).
3) out/external_checks/unicode_sha256.txt — The SHA-256 checksum of the downloaded external/unicode/UnicodeData.txt (store as a single hex line).

Automation and validation:
- Provide a reproducible builder entry point at scripts/build_banner.py that orchestrates the workflow: ensures directories exist, downloads UnicodeData.txt to external/unicode/UnicodeData.txt (skipping if already present and non-empty), parses local inputs, generates out/banner/motd.txt, out/reports/char_report.json, and out/external_checks/unicode_sha256.txt.
- Provide a validation command scripts/validate.sh that I can run locally. It should:
  a) Recompute SHA-256 for external/unicode/UnicodeData.txt and compare to out/external_checks/unicode_sha256.txt.
  b) Check that out/banner/motd.txt exists, begins with the motd_header from input/config.yaml, and contains exactly max_quotes lines formatted with a leading "- ", a bracketed language tag like "[ar]" or "[en]", and an em dash-like separator "—" before the attribution.
  c) Confirm that both "ARABIC" and "LATIN" appear somewhere in external/unicode/UnicodeData.txt (a sanity check that the correct file was fetched), without depending on specific line numbers.
  d) Verify that out/reports/char_report.json contains no unmapped_characters for the selected quotes.
  e) Print a short PASS/FAIL summary and exit with code 0 on success and non-zero on any failure.

Constraints:
- Do not require administrator privileges or write outside this workspace. Do not use /etc; treat out/banner/motd.txt as the motd-like artifact I can later deploy.
- The Internet download step must be essential (no bundling Unicode data locally). Use only the official unicode.org domain for UnicodeData.txt.
- All paths mentioned above are required.

After implementation, I should be able to run:
- python3 scripts/build_banner.py
- bash scripts/validate.sh
and see the expected artifacts and a passing validation. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
